ParsedQuestion: SBI PO Pre 5th Aug 2025  Memory Based
Directions (1-9):Read the following passage and answer the given questions.
The travel and tourism industry has traditionally been a significant contributor to the global economy. Prior to the COVID-19 pandemic, it accounted for nearly 10.4% of the world’s GDP and supported millions of jobs across sectors such as airlines, hotels, transport, and tourism agencies. Continuous growth in both domestic and international travel ensured sustained profits for many companies.
This momentum was abruptly halted with the outbreak of COVID-19 in early 2020. As lockdowns, border closures, and travel bans were enforced worldwide, the industry suffered severe losses. Global passenger traffic declined by over 60% that year. The financial impact was especially harsh on small and medium-sized operators, many of whom had never faced such disruption in the last two decades.
A compelling example is SunWings Travels, a regional bus service provider. Before the pandemic, the company was struggling with poor occupancy — only 62 passengers per week on average. Operations ceased entirely during the lockdown, 	pushing 	the 	business 	toward 	potential shutdown.
However, SunWings made a bold decision to revamp its model. The management handled the crisis with commendable poise, focusing on long-term viability rather than short-term losses. They invested in contactless ticketing, improved air filtration systems, and implemented strict hygiene protocols. The company also trained staff and collaborated with certified lodging partners to ensure safety.
These efforts aligned with the growing demand for safe and reliable road travel. As air travel remained limited, __________________ regional transport options. By the end of 2022, SunWings had more than doubled its weekly passengers and saw a 35% increase in revenue compared to its pre-COVID figures. Encouraged by this turnaround, the company further increased investment in fleet expansion and digital infrastructure.
The post-COVID travel business reflects a shift in priorities: safety, flexibility, and digitisation now dominate consumer preferences. Companies that responded with innovation and resilience, like SunWings Travels, have not only recovered but expanded. The pandemic served as a test of adaptability, and those who navigated it with strategic foresight are now leading the road to recovery.

What was the primary factor that led to the significant decline in global passenger traffic in 2020?

options.text: The closure of all transportation-related businesses due to policy failures
Travel bans, lockdowns, and global restrictions enforced during the pandemic
Reduced interest in traveling due to the rise in domestic job opportunities
Massive protests by tourism employees that halted services temporarily

An unprecedented surge in international airfare pricing post-March 2020

questions.question_text:
ParsedQuestion: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks.

Though the solution seemed ______, it had severe ______ on the long-term budget.
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is (b) simple, implications.

• Simple means easy or uncomplicated (सरल), and

implications means consequences or effects (पररणाम).

• The sentence shows contrast: seemed easy but had long-term impacts.

Example:
• 	His idea was simple and effective.

• 	The new rule has financial implications.

Why Other Options Fail:
• 	(a) Complications → doesn’t match “beneficial”.

• 	(c) Suggestions → not synonymous with "impacts". • 	(d) Effects → too vague; Vague/simple doesn’t contrast 	well.

• 	(e) Procedures → irrelevant to financial outcome.

questions.question_text:
ParsedQuestion: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks.

The journalist’s report was ______ researched and delivered in a ______ manner.
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is (b) thoroughly, lucid.

• Thoroughly means completely and in detail (पूरीिरहसे), and

lucid means clear and easy to understand (स्पष्ट).

• The sentence praises the journalist’s careful work and clarity.

Example:
• 	He thoroughly investigated the matter.

• 	Her explanation was lucid and helpful.

Why Other Options Fail:
• 	(a) Hurriedly/unbiased – negative tone.

• 	(c) Randomly → lacks method; Aggressive → wrong 	tone.

• 	(d) Poorly/responsible – Both are opposite of each 	other.

• 	(e) Selectively/Unclear – implies biased or unclear 	reporting.

questions.question_text:
ParsedQuestion: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No replacement required’.

By the time she will reach the station, the train will have left.

options.text: she would have reached the statin

questions.explanation:
Option (a) is correct.

The correct form is: By the time she reaches the station, the train will have left.

This sentence involves the future perfect tense which is paired with a simple present tense in the time clause. The phrase “By the time” takes present simple tense (not future) even though it refers to the future.

Grammar rule used:

In time clauses introduced by when, before, after, until, or by the time, we use present simple tense to refer to the future, not future tense.

Correct sentence: 
By the time she reaches the station, the train will have left.

Example:
• 	By the time you finish your homework, I will have 	cooked dinner.

Why other options fail:
• 	(b) “had reached” = past perfect, doesn't fit a future 	context.

• 	(c) “would have reached” = conditional, incorrect 	structure.

• 	(d) “is reaching” = present continuous, not idiomatic 	here.

• 	(e) No replacement required → wrong because "will 	reach" is incorrect in time clauses.

questions.question_text:
ParsedQuestion: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No replacement required’.

Neither of the boys have finished their homework yet.
questions.explanation:
Option (a) is correct.

The correct sentence is: “Neither of the boys has finished his homework yet.” 
 Grammar Rule Used:

Correct Sentence: 
Neither of the boys has finished his homework yet.

Example Sentences: 
• 	Neither of the answers is correct.

• 	Neither of the girls has submitted her form.

Why Other Options Are Incorrect: 
• 	(b) have finished his → Verb “have” is plural, but 	“neither” is singular.

• 	(c) has finished their → “Has” is correct, but “their” is 	plural; pronoun mismatch.

• 	(d) has finished her → “Her” is incorrect; the subject is 	boys, so the pronoun should be his.

• 	(e) No replacement required → Incorrect because 	“have” is wrong for a singular subject like “neither”.

Moreover, local farming strengthens the economy by keeping money within the community and creating jobs. However, small farmers frequently struggle to compete with large agricultural corporations due to
________ access to resources and subsidies. Additionally, climate change and unpredictable weather patterns have added another layer of

Urban farming and community gardens have emerged as popular solutions, allowing city dwellers to reconnect with their food sources. questions.explanation:
Option (b) is correct.

The correct phrase is: Had I known about the event, I would have attended it.

This sentence is an example of inversion used in third conditional sentences. “Had I known” replaces “If I had known”, and the verb must be in the past participle form: known, not knew.

Grammar rule used:
• 	In third conditionals, we use: 
• 	If + had + past participle, ... would have + past 	participle

13

|

• 	Inversion structure allows dropping “if” and starting 	with “Had”.

• 	The verb after “had” must be in past participle form.

Correct sentence: 
Had I known about the event, I would have attended it.

Example:
• 	If she had studied, she would have passed.

• 	→ Had she studied, she would have passed.

Why other options fail:
• 	(a) “had knew” – knew is simple past, incorrect form. • 	(c) “have known” – incorrect tense for third conditional. • 	(d) “Did I know” – changes meaning, not a conditional. • 
	(e) No replacement → incorrect because “knew” is 	grammatically wrong in inversion.

Detailed Explanation: 
Let’s break down the sentence part by part: 
(A) "As the euphoria of a decade ago turns to ashes,"

Correct.

• 	Grammatically sound.

• 	It uses the present tense "turns" appropriately for a 	current state arising from past events.

(B) "the Tunisian experience has exposed the daunting 	challenges" 
Correct.

“experience.” 
(C) "in achieving political and economic change after" Correct.

• 	Preposition “in” is correctly used before the gerund 	“achieving.” 
• 	The phrase logically connects the cause and effect.

(D) "an authoritarian order has overthrown" Incorrect.

authoritarian order is the doer of the action.

• 	But the intended meaning is that the authoritarian 	order was the one overthrown (i.e., it was removed).

Correct Grammar Rule: 
To indicate that the authoritarian regime was removed, we must use the passive voice: 
• 	Active:He has overthrown the order.

• 	Passive:The order has been overthrown.

Corrected version: 
“...after an authoritarian order has been overthrown.” Final Answer: (d) D

Which of the following should be the FIFHT sentence after rearrangement?
questions.explanation:
Correct Sequence: F – E – B – C – A – D 
Explanation and Logic: 
(F) – This opens the paragraph with a general overview, introducing the main theme: the modern increase in sugar consumption and its growing health concern.

(E) – Supports and expands the idea in (F) by explaining how people are unknowingly consuming more sugar—through processed foods and drinks.

(B) – Adds a critical evaluation of sugar, pointing out that despite its popularity, sugar lacks nutrients and contributes empty calories. This logically follows (E), reinforcing the health concern.

(C) – Introduces a specific health issue: how sugar impacts metabolism and insulin, directly leading into the harmful physiological effects.

(A) – Naturally follows (C), listing the long-term health consequences like obesity and heart disease, tying individual effects to chronic conditions.

(D) – Acts as the conclusion, offering a solution and summarizing that reducing sugar intake can improve health—closing the paragraph on a forward-looking note.
